--- iter 4 bash --- $ cat <<'EOF' STACK: Werkzeug/3.1.8 on Python/3.9.25, likely Flask app directly serving HTML (no proxy observed), XML upload/parsing functionality. SURFACE: GET / (home with link /addbook and XML example textarea), GET/POST likely /addbook (XML input), common probes (/robots.txt /api /admin /debug /healthz /login /search /sitemap.xml /.git/HEAD /backup) all 404. BUG-HYP: XML upload parser at /addbook may allow external entity expansion / file disclosure. WHY: landing page explicitly offers "Upload a book in XML format" and challenge category is xxe; Werkzeug/Python apps often use lxml/ElementTree parser behavior varies; need wire proof from /addbook form and responses. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: XML submitted to /addbook is parsed with entity resolution enabled (evidence: / advertises XML upload and sample book schema). - ATTACK CHAIN: map /addbook form -> submit baseline XML -> submit XXE reading local files/env -> extract flag. - FIRST PROBE: curl -is http://127.0.0.1:9101/addbook - ABANDON IF: /addbook is absent or parser rejects all DTD/entity use without differentials/errors; then pivot to route discovery/source leaks.
